name: RADIATION INJURY TO BONE

demographics.Location: mandible, ribs, clavicle, humerus, spine, pelvis, femur

dominant_finding: increased intensity of spinal bone marrow on T1WI + T2WI corresponding to radiation
distribution: mandible, ribs, clavicle, humerus, spine, pelvis, femur
discriminating_features: homogeneous high-intensity signal within radiation port on T1WI
occasionally bandlike appearance characterized by peripheral zone of low SI (red
joint space widening ← cartilage hypertrophy (after 8-10 months)
growth plate widening in 1-2 months, often returning to normal by 6 months
permanent alteration in bone length / size ← premature fusion of physis

radiolucency confined to radiation field with narrow zone of transition
increased fragility with sclerosis (= pathologic insufficiency fracture)
